operating_state: Total revenues increased 23% to $82.7 billion driven by DPEP recovery from COVID-19 capacity restrictions with domestic parks at full capacity and DTC subscription growth reaching 164.2 million Disney+ subscribers globally. DMED revenues grew modestly with Direct-to-Consumer gains offset by lower Content Sales/Licensing from strategic shift to internal DTC distribution. Hurricane Ian forced Walt Disney World closure for two days in September 2022.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased with DPEP costs rising from higher labor costs and infrastructure spending aligned with attendance recovery, while DMED programming costs increased reflecting expanded content slate. Content amortization methodology shifted in Q4 2022 with accelerated early-period recognition, while pension expense sensitivity increased significantly with discount rate rising to 5.44% from 2.88%.
material_events: Robert Iger returned as CEO on November 20, 2022 through December 2024, replacing Robert Chapek with mandate to restructure operations and develop succession plan. The company announced plans to reorganize Disney Media and Entertainment Distribution to advance DTC strategies and rationalize costs. Restructuring charges reflected asset impairments related to Russian business exit, while Content License Early Termination reduced revenue as company reclaimed content rights for DTC services.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 continues impacting operations through capacity restrictions at international parks, theater closures, and production disruptions requiring incremental health and safety costs. Foreign exchange headwinds affected international operations with unfavorable impacts on Disney+ international revenue. Enhanced trade restrictions and sanctions against Russia directly impacted operations, while ongoing international political developments threaten theme park investments globally.
forward_outlook: Management expects fiscal 2023 content spending in low $30 billion range and capital expenditures up to $6.7 billion versus $4.9 billion in fiscal 2022. Iger anticipated to initiate organizational changes including potential restructuring of DMED distribution approach within coming months. The company maintains strategic focus on DTC growth with continued subscriber expansion while retaining flexibility to suspend dividends or reduce capital spending if needed.
uncertainty_profile: Pending organizational restructuring under new leadership could result in impairment charges as business strategy undergoes evaluation. DTC business profitability timeline remains uncertain with continued losses expected despite subscriber growth targets. Collective bargaining agreements across operations create ongoing labor dispute exposure, while regulatory landscape remains unpredictable with new compliance requirements across privacy, content licensing, and international trade restrictions.
